content: Just as the language learning sense of reflexion (reflection) requires learners to connect new vocabulary and writing practices to their prior lived experience to deepen comprehension, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be adapted to leverage this educational principle: instead of only contrasting inconsistent solution outputs, LLMs should be trained to ground their self-reflection in accumulated dynamic memory of prior problem-solving traces, similar to how human learners refine understanding through iterative personal reflection. This cross-pollination creates a new hybrid self-reflection framework that bridges human learning strategy and AI agent design.
